content.description:
Hobson's symbol introspection system — REPL functions, cross-item search, and a visual browser for discovering and navigating code symbols across the system.

## REPL Introspection Functions

[introspect-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) provides four global functions registered at boot:

- **`dir(obj)`** — Enumerate an object's properties grouped by kind (functions, sub-namespaces, values). E.g. `dir(api)` lists all methods; `dir(api.viewport)` lists viewport methods. Pure runtime reflection.
- **`await help(obj, 'methodName')`** — Show JSDoc description, signature, params, return type, and source navigation command. Resolves source items via module provenance WeakMap (for libraries) or kernel API special-casing (for `api`, `api.events`, etc.).
- **`await symbols(nameOrId)`** — Dump the `_symbols` map for a code item. E.g. `await symbols('generic-view')` shows all its functions/classes with signatures.
- **`await symbolSearch(pattern, options?)`** — Cross-item symbol search. Pattern can be a string (case-insensitive substring) or RegExp. Options: `{ kind?, scope?, limit = 50 }`. Returns results grouped by item.

## Symbol Extraction & Indexing

- [symbol-extractor-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) parses code items with Acorn and extracts functions, classes, methods, variables, and object literal properties with name, kind, signature, line/column, and JSDoc (description, `@param`, `@returns`).
- [symbol-indexer-watch](item://ac0ac0ac-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) auto-indexes on `item:created` and `item:updated`, storing results in `item.content._symbols`. Only re-saves when symbols actually changed.
- Symbol navigation via `api.navigate(id, { symbol: 'name' })` scrolls to a symbol in code views.
- Symbol transclusion via `![](item://id?symbol=fn)` embeds specific symbols in markdown.

## Symbol Browser View

The [Symbol Browser](item://ac0ac0ac-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) provides a visual UI (rendered by the [symbol-browser-view](item://ac0ac0ac-0007-0000-0000-000000000000)):

- Searchable text input (debounced) across all code items
- Quick filter chips by kind: All, Functions, Classes, Methods, Properties
- Results grouped by item, showing symbol name, signature, kind badge, line number, and JSDoc details
- Click a symbol to navigate to its source via `api.navigate(itemId, { symbol })`
- Persists query and filter state in the browser item

## Kernel API Documentation

The kernel API (`api.*`) is discoverable through the same mechanisms as userland code:

- **JSDoc comments** on all `createAPI()` methods in [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) provide descriptions, `@param` types, and `@returns` — 168 JSDoc comments across the four kernel modules.
- **Symbol extraction** walks object literal properties inside `createAPI()`, so all `api.*` methods appear in kernel:core's `_symbols`.
- **Provenance** — `help()` special-cases `api` → kernel:core (since `api` isn't loaded via `require()`), and recognizes sub-namespaces (`api.events`, `api.viewport`, `api.instances`, `api.helpers`).

## Module Provenance

[ModuleSystem](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000) tracks which item each loaded module came from via a `WeakMap` (`moduleMetadata`). After `evaluateCodeItem()`, it stores `{ itemId, itemName }` keyed by the module object. `help()` uses this to resolve any `require()`'d module back to its source item — no manual metadata, works for all libraries automatically.

WeakMap is necessary because ES module namespace objects are non-extensible by spec. It also provides automatic garbage collection when modules are evicted from cache.

## Items

| Item | ID | Role |
|------|----|------|
| [introspect-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) | `ac0ac0ac-0005` | REPL functions: dir, help, symbols, symbolSearch |
| [symbol-extractor-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) | `ac0ac0ac-0003` | Acorn-based extraction with JSDoc parsing |
| [symbol-indexer-watch](item://ac0ac0ac-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) | `ac0ac0ac-0004` | Auto-indexes on item create/update |
| [symbol-browser-view](item://ac0ac0ac-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) | `ac0ac0ac-0007` | Visual searchable symbol browser |
| [symbol-browser type](item://ac0ac0ac-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) | `ac0ac0ac-0006` | Type definition for browser instances |
